Redesigning
LinkedIn
for Clarity

A human-centered redesign of LinkedIn's job search experience, from user research and ideation through two rounds of functional prototyping.

Methods
Design Thinking, Usability Testing, Prototyping
LinkedIn's job search experience fails its most anxious users. Graduate students navigating the job market face a platform designed for established professionals, leaving them confused, anxious, and in the dark after every application. This project set out to change that.
What LinkedIn's Users Actually Feel

Listening before building

We conducted 5 in-depth user interviews spanning a spectrum from first-time LinkedIn users to active power users, to understand real pain points before touching a single design decision.

Click each card to uncover what users told us. Once all four are revealed, watch how each insight connects to a design question.

01
The Posting Paradox
tap to reveal
"I don't post often, only when semester ends. I procrastinate." — User A
"Fear of being unprofessional stops her from posting." — User B
"Algorithm unpredictability makes posting feel risky." — User C
02
The Algorithm Doesn't Know Me
tap to reveal
"The platform doesn't use my profile to match me to the right jobs." — User D
"Job filters are irrelevant, when you filter, more jobs still appear." — User A
"Unclear job titles, hard to know what roles fit me." — User E
03
More Than a Job Board
tap to reveal
"My day starts scrolling LinkedIn instead of Instagram." — User A
"I treated LinkedIn like a long-term branding project." — User C
"Can get guidance from connections about the company and interviewer." — User D
04
The Trust and Safety Gap
tap to reveal
"Job posting scam leads you to some fake website." — User A
"Random people were using my photo on their feed. Freaked me out." — User A
"LinkedIn transfers applications to 3rd party sites." — User B

Every theme maps to a design question

Algorithm unpredictability makes posting feel risky
Job filters are irrelevant, more jobs still appear
The platform doesn't use my profile effectively to match me to the right jobs. -- User D
Job posting scam leads to fake websites
How might we reduce friction in applying and engaging on LinkedIn?
How might we reduce confusion in LinkedIn's job recommendations?
How might we make users feel understood by LinkedIn's algorithm?
How might we ensure trust, safety, and accuracy of opportunities?
Primary Persona

Meet Maya

Maya
Graduate Student -- The Anxious Applicant
Student, actively job searching with high urgency as graduation approaches
Low confidence, fears judgment, overthinks every post and application
Applied to many jobs with little to no response, stuck in the black hole
Unclear on which parts of her profile actually matter to recruiters
Knows networking works but doesn't know how to leverage it effectively
Why Maya?

Maya represents the largest and most underserved segment of LinkedIn's users, graduate students navigating the platform during the most stressful period of their lives. Designing for Maya means designing for clarity, trust, and confidence.

What If We...

From insight to idea

From 25 ideas generated across 5 personas and 5 HMW statements, 4 concepts rose to the top and became the foundation of our first prototype.

01
Application Journey Tracker
Show each stage of the hiring process and what to expect next, eliminating the post-application black hole.
02
Profile Match Score
Show users exactly how well their profile matches a job, skills percentage, experience alignment, gaps to address.
03
One-Click Easy Apply
Frictionless application with auto-filled, editable profile data. Reduce the overthinking barrier for anxious users.
04
Community Insights
Surface real applicant timelines and recruiter responsiveness, peer-verified, not platform-generated.
Maya's Story

Visualized before built

Before prototyping, we mapped Maya's emotional journey from graduation anxiety to confidence in an 8-panel storyboard.

Our First Bet

Building the first version

We translated our top ideas into a functional HTML prototype, Maya's full job search journey from browsing to post-application clarity.

Version 1
Initial Prototype
A 5-screen journey covering job browsing, job details with Community Insights, Easy Apply, Application Tracking, and Maya's Clarity screen.
Open Prototype v1
Community Insights Application Tracker Easy Apply Skill Match Score Maya's Clarity Screen
We're Listening
What users actually told us

4 usability sessions on Prototype v1 revealed 3 consistent patterns that shaped every decision in v2.

Theme 01
Community Insights is the standout feature, but credibility matters
"I was drawn to the Community Insights first, even before the job details." -- User A
"This kind of peer information makes the process feel less uncertain." -- User A
"Wanted proof that profiles are real, not fake accounts." -- User D
Theme 02
Application tracking reduces anxiety, but needs more depth
"Still feels like a black hole." -- User A
"I want clearer timing for each step so I know how long I'm expected to wait." -- User B
"Wants probability or likelihood indicators per stage." -- User A
Theme 03
Seeing other applicants is useful, but creates comparison stress
"Seeing other applicants' information is helpful, but it also makes you compare yourself a bit." -- User A
"Can be helpful for comparison, but it can also feel stressful." -- User B
We Heard You

4 changes. Driven by evidence.

Every change in v2 directly addresses a pattern from usability testing. Nothing added for aesthetics alone.

01
Verified Badges in Community Insights
Each contributor shows university and graduation year, building trust and eliminating fake profile anxiety.
02
Enhanced Application Tracker
Stage-specific timing estimates, a likelihood indicator, and notifications when similar applicants advance.
03
Smarter Job Filters
Results accurately reflect selected criteria. No more irrelevant postings slipping through after filtering.
04
Recruiter Credibility Score
A tiered Gold/Silver/Bronze score showing response rate, process completion, and average response time, visible before applying.
Version 2
Feedback-Driven Iteration
All 4 improvements built into a fully functional prototype. Experience the difference firsthand.
Open Prototype v2
Verified Community Badges Enhanced Tracker with Timing Smarter Job Filters Recruiter Credibility Score Likelihood Indicator Notification Prompts
